question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 (P1) removes `address-family ipv6 unicast` from `router isis core`.
R3 stops being an IPv6 IS-IS speaker. The R2↔R3 and R3↔R4 IS-IS
adjacencies drop on the IPv6 AF. R3 stops advertising/transiting IPv6
routes. PE1↔PE2 IPv6 underlay (fc00::2 ↔ fc00::4) becomes unreachable
through R3; if no alternate IPv6 path exists, VPNv4 over IPv6 dies.
In this lab R3 is the only P-router between PE1 and PE2, so this is
a definitive partition.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.103 (R3, P1), address-family ipv6 unicast was removed from router isis core, so R3 ceases to be an IPv6 IS-IS speaker. Without the IPv6 AF, IS-IS cannot exchange IPv6 reachability over the R2<->R3 and R3<->R4 links, so R3 no longer advertises or transits IPv6 routes.
Because R3 is the only P-router between PE1 and PE2, removing it from the IPv6 IGP partitions the IPv6 underlay (fc00::2 <-> fc00::4), and with no alternate IPv6 path the VPNv4-over-IPv6 service loses its transport and traffic blackholes.
Fix: restore the IS-IS IPv6 AF on R3 (metric-style wide + SRv6 locator TRANSIT, plus the Loopback0/Gi0/0/0/0/Gi0/0/0/1 IPv6 AF bindings) as in the revert.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; removing
the IS-IS IPv6 AF doesn't emit an ADJCHANGE log in this profile,
but the underlay-reachability collapse is visible via CLI probe.
Diagnostic ground truth lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R3 `show running-config router isis core`: no `address-family ipv6 unicast` block (baseline has it alongside SRv6 locator TRANSIT).
R2 and R4 `show isis neighbors`: R3 no longer listed under the IPv6 AF; the IPv4-only L2 entry may persist depending on platform behaviour.
R3 `show segment-routing srv6 locator`: TRANSIT entry gone (its only IGP wiring was through the now-removed IPv6 AF).
Any router `show route ipv6 fc00::4`: Network not in table — R3 is the only P-router so PE1↔PE2 IPv6 underlay is partitioned.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis core
 no address-family ipv6 unicast

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis core
 address-family ipv6 unicast
  metric-style wide
  segment-routing srv6
   locator TRANSIT
!
router isis core
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv6 unicast
!
router isis core
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 point-to-point
 address-family ipv6 unicast
!
router isis core
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 point-to-point
 address-family ipv6 unicast
